headline: Bill Ackman Invests $1.27 Billion in Amazon, Adds To Google and More
summary: Bill Ackman has made a hefty investment in Amazon, pouring in more than $1.27 billion within a span of three months.

headline: Elon Musk's xAI To Simulate Software Giants Like Microsoft, Calling It 'Macrohard'
summary: Elon Musk has announced plans to simulate software companies such as Microsoft using AI.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Sunrun, Newegg, Walmart — And Powell Offers Rate Cut Hope
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors' favorite stocks over the last week — here's a look at some of our top stories.

headline: Meta To Launch First Consumer Smart Glasses With Display, Gesture-Control Wristband At Connect Conference: Report
summary: Meta is set to unveil its first display-equipped smart glasses next month, expanding its partnership with EssilorLuxottica and advancing its augmented reality push with new hardware and gesture-based tech.

headline: Amazon Partnership Sends Hertz Stock Up 8% As US Pre-Owned Car Market Set To Hit $1 Trillion By 2030
summary: Hertz shares jumped 8.57% after announcing a partnership with Amazon to sell certified pre-owned cars online, expanding their digital reach in the growing used car market.

headline: Kimbal Musk Defends Brother Elon's $29 Billion Tesla Pay Deal, Says CEO 'Deserves To Be Paid' After Years With Zero Salary
summary: Tesla board member Kimbal Musk defended CEO Elon Musk's newly approved $29 billion compensation package, saying his brother "deserves to be paid" after years without a salary, even as the deal faces regulatory scrutiny, legal challenges, and investor debate.

headline: Apple Reportedly Eyes Google Gemini To Overhaul Siri As Analysts Slam Tim Cook's AI Strategy As 'Disaster'
summary: Apple is reportedly in talks with Google to use its Gemini AI models to overhaul Siri, as analysts criticize Tim Cook's AI strategy as a "disaster" and urge Apple to pursue acquisitions and partnerships to catch up with rivals.

headline: 'Trump Wasn't Hired To Run A Hedge Fund,' Says Peter Schiff As Intel Deal Divides Experts Amid Calls To Break Taiwan Dependence For Chips
summary: President Donald Trump's announcement that the U.S. government now holds a 10% stake in Intel, valued at $11 billion, has sparked sharp debate, with economist Peter Schiff calling it unconstitutional and a threat to free-market capitalism, while analyst Daniel Newman praised it as a critical investment for national security and semiconductor independence.

headline: Palantir CEO Alex Karp Dumps $63 Million In Stock As PLTR Surges 111% This Year
summary: Palantir CEO Alex Karp sold $62.7 million in stock amid rising valuation concerns and insider selling pressure, even as the stock remains up 397% year-over-year.
